description:
Establish the complete data persistence layer for the identity-service. This epic covers all configuration, schema, and ORM work required before authentication and authorization features can be built. It spans service profile configuration, Flyway-managed schema creation for users, roles, user_roles, and refresh_tokens, JPA entity mapping including composite key modeling for the user_roles junction table, repository setup, and the test infrastructure needed to validate migration and entity alignment without requiring a running database.

Stories in this epic are sourced from:
- story-identity-application-properties.json
- stories-identity-schema.json (migrations, User and Role entities, UserRole and RefreshToken entities)

The Docker Compose MySQL infrastructure story is excluded — it is tracked under a separate infrastructure epic.
userType: backend developer owning the Identity and Access Service
functionality: configure the service's data layer including database connectivity, schema migrations, JPA entities for users, roles, and token management, and test infrastructure
benefit: so the identity service has a validated persistence foundation that authentication, authorization, and token management features can build on without schema drift between environments

stories.summary: Scaffold Identity Service with multi-profile configuration and actuator health endpoints
stories.description: Initialize the identity-service Spring Boot project following the same structure as gateway-service. The service must define default, dev, and prod configuration profiles with actuator health endpoint configuration per profile. A test package with a context-load smoke test must be included. No actuator configuration is applied in the test profile.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Default profile starts with health endpoint only
acceptanceCriteria.given: the identity-service is started with no active profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 response is returned with liveness and readiness probe data; no other actuator endpoints are exposed

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Dev profile exposes all actuator endpoints with full health details
acceptanceCriteria.given: the identity-service is started with the dev profile active
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 response is returned with show-details: always and all actuator endpoints are accessible

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Prod profile file exists and application starts cleanly
acceptanceCriteria.given: the identity-service is started with the prod profile active
acceptanceCriteria.then: the service starts without errors using prod profile overrides

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Test context loads without actuator configuration
acceptanceCriteria.given: the Maven test phase is run
acceptanceCriteria.then: the Spring application context loads successfully with no actuator endpoint exposure

tasks.description: Create pom.xml under backend/identity-service following the gateway-service model: Spring Boot 3.5.13, Java 17, spring-boot-starter-actuator, spring-boot-starter-web, lombok, spring-boot-starter-test (test scope). Include maven-compiler-plugin with Lombok annotation processor path and spring-boot-maven-plugin with Lombok exclusion.

Create src/main/java/com/loanflow/identity/IdentityServiceApplication.java annotated with @SpringBootApplication. Package must follow com.loanflow.identity convention, consistent with gateway-service's com.loanflow.gateway.

Create src/main/resources/application.yaml. Set spring.application.name to identity-service. Configure management.endpoint.health.probes.enabled: true, management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: health, management.health.liveness-state.enabled: true, and management.health.readiness-state.enabled: true. Mirrors the gateway-service default profile.

Create src/main/resources/application-dev.yaml. Set management.endpoint.health.show-details: always, management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: "*", and management.info.env.enabled: true. Mirrors the gateway-service dev profile.

Create src/main/resources/application-prod.yaml as a minimal placeholder. No actuator overrides required at this stage. File must exist to establish the prod profile structure alongside default and dev.

Create src/test/java/com/loanflow/identity/IdentityServiceApplicationTests.java annotated with @SpringBootTest. Add a contextLoads() method annotated with @Test. No actuator configuration should be active in the test context. Package follows com.loanflow.identity.

tasks.summary: Verify service starts on correct port
tasks.description: Confirm the identity-service starts on port 8081 using ./mvnw spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=dev without errors. Verify GET /actuator/health returns 200 with expected liveness and readiness fields.

stories.summary: Configure application properties across base, dev, and test profiles for identity-service
stories.description:
The identity-service is scaffolded with actuator and base profile files in place. This story adds the required Maven dependencies for persistence, migration, and validation, defines environment variables for the identity database, and populates application-dev.yaml and application-test.yaml so the service can start, connect to MySQL, run Flyway migrations, and run tests in isolation against H2 without requiring a running database instance.

Note: infra/mysql/init/01_init.sql must be updated to provision the loanflow_identity_db database and grant the application user access. This depends on the Docker Compose MySQL infrastructure story.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Service starts and runs Flyway migrations on dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container is running and environment variables are set from .env
acceptanceCriteria.when: the identity-service starts with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs migration scripts from classpath:db/migration against loanflow_identity_db and JPA validates the schema without attempting to auto-create tables

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Tests run Flyway migrations against H2 and validate entity mappings
acceptanceCriteria.given: the test profile is active and no MySQL instance is running
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs all migration scripts against H2 in-memory, Hibernate validates entity mappings against the migrated schema, and the context loads without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: No credentials are hardcoded in source
acceptanceCriteria.then: all connection values are resolved from environment variables with inline defaults and no plaintext credentials appear in committed files

tasks.summary: Add JPA, MySQL, Flyway, Validation, and H2 dependencies to pom.xml
tasks.description:
Add the following dependencies to the identity-service pom.xml:

- spring-boot-starter-data-jpa
- spring-boot-starter-validation
- mysql-connector-j (runtime scope)
- flyway-core
- com.h2database:h2 (test scope only)

Ensure versions are compatible with Spring Boot 3.5.13.

tasks.summary: Add identity database environment variables to infra/.env.example
Configure datasource, JPA, and Flyway in application-dev.yaml
tasks.description:
Populate application-dev.yaml with server port, datasource, JPA, and Flyway settings. Each connection part must use environment variable substitution with inline defaults:

server:
  port: 8081

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mysql://${DATABASE_HOST:localhost}:${DATABASE_PORT:3306}/${IDENTITY_DB_NAME:loanflow_identity_db}
    username: ${DATABASE_USER:loanflow}
    password: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD:password}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: true
    properties:
      hibernate:
        dialect: org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect
        format_sql: true
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration

tasks.summary: Configure application-test.yaml to run Flyway against H2 with schema validation
tasks.description:
Populate application-test.yaml so tests run Flyway migrations against H2 in-memory and validate entity mappings against the migrated schema without requiring MySQL or Docker:

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h2:mem:loanflow_identity_test;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;MODE=MySQL;NON_KEYWORDS=VALUE
    driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver
    username: sa
    password:
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
    show-sql: true
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration

Note: migration SQL must be written to the H2-compatible subset. Avoid ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP and ENGINE=InnoDB.

stories.summary: Create Flyway migration scripts for identity service schema
stories.description: Write the SQL migration scripts that define the identity service database schema across four tables: users, roles, user_roles, and refresh_tokens. Migrations run in order via Flyway against MySQL 8 in dev and against H2 in MySQL mode in the test profile. All SQL must be written to the common subset supported by both targets: avoid ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, ENGINE=InnoDB, and MySQL-specific index syntax not supported by H2. Each table is defined in its own versioned file under src/main/resources/db/migration.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Flyway runs all migrations cleanly on dev startup
acceptanceCriteria.given: the MySQL container is running and the identity-service starts with the dev profile
acceptanceCriteria.then: all four migration scripts apply in order without errors and the flyway_schema_history table records each as success

acceptanceCriteria.when: the identity-service starts with ddl-auto set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates all entity mappings against the schema without throwing a validation exception

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Migrations execute against H2 in test profile and entities validate
acceptanceCriteria.given: the test profile is active with Flyway enabled against H2 and ddl-auto set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Flyway runs all migration scripts against H2 without error and Hibernate validates all entity mappings against the migrated schema

tasks.description:
Define the users table:

- user_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- username VARCHAR(150) NOT NULL
- email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
- password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL
- first_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL
- last_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL
- status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL
- created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
- updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- UNIQUE on username
- UNIQUE on email

Indexes:
- INDEX on status

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses. Do not use ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP — updated_at will be managed by Hibernate @UpdateTimestamp.

Define the roles table:

- role_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- role_code VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
- role_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL
- description VARCHAR(255) NULL

Constraints:
- UNIQUE on role_code

Seed data — insert the three base roles as part of this migration:
INSERT INTO roles (role_code, role_name, description) VALUES
  ('APPLICANT', 'Applicant', 'Loan applicant submitting and tracking applications'),
  ('LOAN_OFFICER', 'Loan Officer', 'Internal reviewer managing applications and documents'),
  ('ADMIN', 'Administrator', 'Platform administrator with full access');

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

Define the user_roles junction table with a composite primary key:

- user_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- role_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- assigned_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- PRIMARY KEY (user_id, role_id)
- FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id)
- FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles(role_id)

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

Define the refresh_tokens table:

- refresh_token_id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY
- user_id BIGINT NOT NULL
- token_value VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL
- expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
- revoked BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE
- created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Constraints:
- UNIQUE on token_value
- FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id)

Indexes:
- INDEX on user_id
- INDEX on expires_at

H2 compatibility: omit ENGINE=InnoDB and DEFAULT CHARSET clauses.

stories.summary: Create User and Role JPA entities and repositories
stories.description: Implement the User and Role JPA entities mapped to the users and roles tables, along with their supporting enums and Spring Data repositories. User is the aggregate root for the identity service. The many-to-many relationship between User and Role is owned by User via a join table. All field names, column mappings, and constraints must align with the V1 and V2 Flyway migrations.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: User and Role entities validate against the migrated schema
acceptanceCriteria.given: Flyway migrations have run and ddl-auto is set to validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates User and Role against users and roles without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: User can be persisted with roles
acceptanceCriteria.given: Role records exist from the V2 seed migration
acceptanceCriteria.when: a User is saved with a role assignment
acceptanceCriteria.then: the user and the user_roles join record are persisted and retrievable

acceptanceCriteria.given: a User with a given username or email already exists
acceptanceCriteria.when: a second User is saved with the same username or email

tasks.description:
Create enums under com.loanflow.identityservice.entity.enums:

UserStatus: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, LOCKED

RoleCode: APPLICANT, LOAN_OFFICER, ADMIN

Annotate with @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING) where used in entities to persist the string value rather than ordinal.

Create com.loanflow.identityservice.entity.User mapped to the users table:

- @Id @GeneratedValue userId
- username (VARCHAR 150, unique, non-updatable)
- email (VARCHAR 255, unique)
- passwordHash (VARCHAR 255)
- firstName, lastName
- status (@Enumerated STRING)
- createdAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)
- updatedAt (@UpdateTimestamp)
- roles: @ManyToMany(fetch = FetchType.LAZY) @JoinTable(name = "user_roles", joinColumns = @JoinColumn(name = "user_id"), inverseJoinColumns = @JoinColumn(name = "role_id"))

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "users"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok.

Create com.loanflow.identityservice.entity.Role mapped to the roles table:

- @Id @GeneratedValue roleId
- roleCode (@Enumerated STRING, unique)
- roleName
- description (nullable)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "roles"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok.

Create the following repositories under com.loanflow.identityservice.repository:

UserRepository extending JpaRepository<User, Long>:
- findByUsername(String username) returning Optional<User>
- findByEmail(String email) returning Optional<User>
- existsByUsername(String username) returning boolean
- existsByEmail(String email) returning boolean
- findByStatus(UserStatus status) returning List<User>

RoleRepository extending JpaRepository<Role, Long>:
- findByRoleCode(RoleCode roleCode) returning Optional<Role>

stories.summary: Create UserRole and RefreshToken JPA entities and repositories
stories.description: Implement the UserRole entity with a composite key mapping to the user_roles junction table, and the RefreshToken entity mapping to the refresh_tokens table. RefreshToken supports the login, token refresh, logout, and revocation workflow. All mappings must align with the V3 and V4 Flyway migrations.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: UserRole and RefreshToken entities validate against migrated schema
acceptanceCriteria.given: all four Flyway migrations have run and ddl-auto is validate
acceptanceCriteria.then: Hibernate validates UserRole and RefreshToken without error

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Refresh token can be issued and revoked
acceptanceCriteria.given: a User exists in the database
acceptanceCriteria.when: a RefreshToken is saved and then updated with revoked set to true
acceptanceCriteria.then: the revocation is persisted and the token is retrievable with revoked equals true

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Expired and revoked tokens are queryable
acceptanceCriteria.given: multiple refresh tokens exist with varying expiry and revocation states
acceptanceCriteria.when: the repository is queried for active tokens for a given user
acceptanceCriteria.then: only non-revoked, non-expired tokens are returned

tasks.description:
Create com.loanflow.identityservice.entity.UserRoleId as an @Embeddable composite key:

- userId (BIGINT)
- roleId (BIGINT)

Implement equals() and hashCode() based on both fields. Annotate with @Embeddable, @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @AllArgsConstructor, @EqualsAndHashCode from Lombok.

Create com.loanflow.identityservice.entity.UserRole mapped to the user_roles table:

- @EmbeddedId id (UserRoleId)
- @ManyToOne @MapsId("userId") @JoinColumn(name = "user_id") user
- @ManyToOne @MapsId("roleId") @JoinColumn(name = "role_id") role
- assignedAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "user_roles"), @Getter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok.

Create UserRoleRepository extending JpaRepository<UserRole, UserRoleId>:
- findByUser_UserId(Long userId) returning List<UserRole>
- existsByUser_UserIdAndRole_RoleCode(Long userId, RoleCode roleCode) returning boolean

Create com.loanflow.identityservice.entity.RefreshToken mapped to the refresh_tokens table:

- @Id @GeneratedValue refreshTokenId
- @ManyToOne @JoinColumn(name = "user_id") user
- tokenValue (VARCHAR 500, unique)
- expiresAt (TIMESTAMP, not null)
- revoked (BOOLEAN, default false)
- createdAt (@CreationTimestamp, updatable = false)

Annotate with @Entity, @Table(name = "refresh_tokens"), @Getter, @Setter, @NoArgsConstructor, @Builder from Lombok.

Create RefreshTokenRepository extending JpaRepository<RefreshToken, Long>:
- findByTokenValue(String tokenValue) returning Optional<RefreshToken>
- findByUser_UserIdAndRevokedFalseAndExpiresAtAfter(Long userId, Timestamp now) returning List<RefreshToken>
- revokeAllByUser_UserId(Long userId) — @Modifying @Query to set revoked = true for all tokens belonging to a user (used on logout)
